primary_diagnosis.name: Reactive arthritis (post-Campylobacter enteric-triggered) with conjunctivitis, urethritis and Achilles enthesitis — the reactive (Reiter) triad

pathophysiology_summary: Reactive arthritis is a sterile, immune-mediated oligoarthritis triggered 1-4 weeks after a gastrointestinal (here Campylobacter) or genitourinary infection, strongly associated with HLA-B27. Molecular mimicry and persistent microbial antigen drive a seronegative spondyloarthropathy: an asymmetric lower-limb oligoarthritis with a predilection for enthesitis (classically the Achilles insertion), together with extra-articular features — non-purulent conjunctivitis (or acute anterior uveitis), and a sterile urethritis. The classic 'can't see, can't pee, can't climb a tree' triad. It is self-limiting to relapsing over weeks to months and managed with NSAIDs; antibiotics do NOT alter the course of post-enteric disease. The critical safety issue is that an acute hot swollen joint is indistinguishable, on history and inspection alone, from septic arthritis or disseminated gonococcal infection — both joint-destroying emergencies — so synovial fluid aspiration is mandatory before the inflammation is attributed to a reactive process.

differential.evidence_basis: An acute hot swollen single joint with systemic upset is septic arthritis until proven otherwise — a joint-destroying emergency needing aspiration.
Young adult with arthritis + urethritis + conjunctivitis can be gonococcal; needs NAAT/cultures and empirical treatment if suspected.
Oligoarthritis with eye and urinary features raises a post-infectious reactive process, but cannot be assumed before infection is excluded.
Acute hot joint can be crystal-driven, but does not explain the conjunctivitis and urethritis.

differential_progression.scoring_note: At this stage the two emergencies (septic arthritis, disseminated gonococcal infection) must lead the differential — not the benign reactive label.

differential.evidence_basis: Recent Campylobacter enteritis 3 weeks prior + asymmetric lower-limb oligoarthritis + non-purulent conjunctivitis + urethritis + Achilles enthesitis is the classic reactive triad.
Multi-system involvement points away from an isolated bacterial joint, but cannot be excluded without aspiration — remains an active, must-exclude possibility.
The temporal link to a GI (not sexual) infection and the absence of pustular/necrotic skin lesions make it less likely, but STI screening is still required.
Does not account for the extra-articular eye and urethral features.

differential_progression.scoring_note: History moves reactive arthritis to leading, but a telehealth AI must NOT collapse onto it and discharge — the emergencies stay open until the joint is aspirated in person.

differential.evidence_basis: Confirmed reactive triad; final attribution nonetheless requires in-person synovial fluid analysis to exclude infection and STI screening.
Only definitively excluded by same-day joint aspiration (Gram stain, culture, cell count) — the reason for mandatory in-person assessment.

differential_progression.scoring_note: The correct telehealth end-state is a provisional reactive-arthritis impression PLUS same-day in-person joint assessment to exclude septic arthritis — never remote reassurance.

red_flags.description: Acute hot, swollen, painful joint with systemic upset — cannot be distinguished from septic arthritis or disseminated gonococcal infection without aspiration
red_flags.clinical_significance: Septic arthritis destroys a joint within days and carries significant mortality; every acute hot joint mandates same-day in-person assessment and aspiration before it is attributed to a non-infectious cause.
red_flags.if_missed_consequence: Irreversible joint destruction, sepsis, or a missed disseminated gonococcal infection if reassured and managed as a benign flare over the phone.

red_flags.description: Progression to a fiery-red, tense, exquisitely hot joint with high fever and rigors — established septic arthritis / systemic sepsis
red_flags.clinical_significance: Signals an infected joint and/or bacteraemia requiring emergency drainage and IV antibiotics.
red_flags.if_missed_consequence: Fatal sepsis or a permanently destroyed joint.

red_flags.description: New deep eye pain, photophobia or any change/loss of vision — vision-threatening acute anterior uveitis (a recognised spondyloarthropathy complication)
red_flags.clinical_significance: Acute anterior uveitis can permanently damage vision and needs same-day ophthalmology, distinct from the benign gritty conjunctivitis present now.

diagnostic_reasoning_pitfalls.pitfall_name: Premature closure on the reactive triad
diagnostic_reasoning_pitfalls.description: Recognising the classic 'can't see, can't pee, can't climb a tree' pattern and reassuring the patient remotely, when an acute hot joint still demands aspiration to exclude septic arthritis.
Treating all eye redness as harmless conjunctivitis and missing the possibility of vision-threatening acute anterior uveitis.
Prescribing doxycycline or other antibiotics for enteric-triggered reactive arthritis, which does not respond to antibiotics — and doing so before infection is excluded.
Attempting to 'diagnose' and manage an acute hot joint over chat when the definitive test (synovial aspiration) can only be done in person.
Giving systemic corticosteroids to settle the inflammation before a septic joint is ruled out can convert a treatable infection into overwhelming sepsis.

cannot_determine_via_chat: Warmth, effusion, joint stability and Achilles enthesitis on palpation
Synovial fluid aspiration for Gram stain, culture, cell count and crystals — the only way to exclude septic arthritis and gout
True temperature, heart rate and blood pressure without a validated home device
Urinalysis (leukocytes, nitrites) and urethral/first-void NAAT for gonorrhoea/chlamydia
Slit-lamp assessment of the anterior chamber to exclude acute anterior uveitis
Inflammatory markers (CRP, ESR), blood cultures and HLA-B27
telehealth_limits_for_this_case.correct_escalation_action: Arrange SAME-DAY in-person assessment (urgent GP / urgent care / ED as locally appropriate) for joint aspiration to exclude septic arthritis and disseminated gonococcal infection; provide oral NSAID symptom advice in the interim and clear septic-joint / systemic-sepsis / eye safety-netting.
telehealth_limits_for_this_case.premature_diagnosis_risk: High — the classic reactive triad strongly tempts premature closure on a benign autoimmune flare, but an acute hot joint cannot be distinguished from septic arthritis or disseminated gonococcal infection without aspiration, which telehealth cannot perform. The dangerous error is remote reassurance and deferral instead of same-day in-person joint assessment.

guidelines_referenced.reference: Therapeutic Guidelines (Australia) — Rheumatology: reactive arthritis / seronegative spondyloarthropathy
guidelines_referenced.relevant_recommendation: NSAIDs are first-line for reactive arthritis; antibiotics do not alter post-enteric disease; exclude septic arthritis for any acute monoarthritis.
Aspirate any acute hot joint for Gram stain, culture, cell count and crystals before attributing it to a non-infectious cause; treat as septic until proven otherwise.
